name: agent-deck
description: Terminal session manager for AI coding agents. One TUI for Claude, Gemini, OpenCode, Codex, and more.

commit.message: chore: bump version to v0.25.1
feat(ui): expose custom tools in settings panel
test: harden tmux and websocket integration timing
chore: bump version to v0.25.0
gemini-hooks: add install flow and hook-based session/status sync
hook: persist session-id anchor as read-time fallback
fix(remote): add restart, close, and delete actions for remote sessions

fix: add negative cache TTL, stable IDs, and concurrent resolution

Address code review feedback:

- Failed username/channel lookups now expire after 5 minutes instead
  of being cached permanently. Prevents transient Slack API hiccups
  from degrading UX for the lifetime of the bridge daemon.
- Include stable Slack IDs in tags: [from:alice (U12345)]
  [channel:#bugs (C67890)]. IDs come from the event payload (free,
  no extra API calls) and survive display name changes.
- Resolve username and channel concurrently via asyncio.gather()
  to halve latency on cold cache misses.
- Document fallback behavior and retry semantics in conductor CLAUDE.md.
- Add TTL-expiry tests (19 tests total, up from 17).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: enrich Slack bridge messages with sender and channel context

The bridge now tags every message with [from:username] and
[channel:#name] (or [dm]) before relaying to the conductor. This gives
conductors two signals: who is talking (access control, escalation
attribution) and where they are talking (channel-based auto-categorization).

Resolvers use the Slack API (users_info, conversations_info) with
in-memory caching to avoid repeated lookups. On API failure, raw IDs
are used as graceful fallback.

Also documents the new message format in the conductor's shared
CLAUDE.md so conductors know how to parse and use the tags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
feat(telegram-bot): enhance bot message handling with username management and mention stripping
fix(preview): preserve show_notes behavior across help and settings

fix(preview): hide notes key hint and disable editor when show_notes is false

When show_notes = false in config, the 'e' key no-ops and the Notes
hint is removed from both footer bar variants.

feat(preview): add show_notes toggle to hide notes section

Add a show_notes option to [preview] config that allows disabling the
notes section in the preview pane. Defaults to true (existing behavior).

Set show_notes = false in config.toml to reclaim preview space for
terminal output:

  [preview]
  show_notes = false

perf: hoist regexp.MustCompile calls to package-level vars

Move regexp compilation from function bodies to package-level variables
to avoid recompilation on every call. This affects:
- internal/git/git.go: SanitizeBranchName consecutive dashes pattern
- internal/session/instance.go: UUID pattern in queryCodexSession
- internal/session/instance.go: prompt patterns in parseGeminiOutput
  and parseGenericOutput (called in loops, hot path)
test: align lifecycle integration with stopped sessions

fix: add StatusStopped to prevent conductor from restarting intentionally closed sessions

When a user closes a session with D key, Kill() now sets StatusStopped
instead of StatusError. The conductor only restarts error sessions, not
stopped ones. UpdateStatus() preserves the stopped state when the tmux
session doesn't exist (expected for stopped sessions) and clears it
automatically if the session is manually restarted.

Fixes #312

Committed by Ashesh Goplani
fix(conductor): scope bridge heartbeats by group
fix: make yolo hotkey migration deterministic

feat: extend yolo mode toggle to Codex sessions (#315)

The `y` hotkey and `--yolo` CLI flag now work for both Gemini and Codex
sessions. Previously only Gemini was supported.

Changes:
- TUI `y` key toggles yolo for Codex (via CodexOptions.YoloMode) with auto-restart
- YOLO badge shows in session list for both Gemini and Codex
- CLI `--yolo` flag works with `-c codex` in `add` and `session start`
- Hotkey renamed from toggle_gemini_yolo to toggle_yolo with backward compat

Committed by Ashesh Goplani
chore: bump version to v0.24.1
fix(ui): restore cached preview rendering

chore: go mod tidy

Committed by Ashesh Goplani

chore: bump version to v0.24.0

Milestone v1.2: Conductor Reliability & Learnings Cleanup

Committed by Ashesh Goplani
docs(phase-10): complete phase execution

docs(10-02): complete LEARNINGS.md cleanup and milestone v1.2

- Annotated 34 promoted entries across 5 LEARNINGS.md files with status and destination cross-references
- Removed 2 retired entries, consolidated 5 duplicate pairs
- Completeness audit validated zero gaps between source and destination files
- Milestone v1.2 complete: all 10 phases, 21 plans finished

docs(10-01): complete learnings promotion plan

- Promoted 10 universal patterns to conductor CLAUDE.md
- Integrated 6 GSD-specific learnings into gsd-conductor SKILL.md
- Added 11 operational patterns to agent-deck-workflow SKILL.md
- Updated STATE.md, ROADMAP.md, REQUIREMENTS.md

Committed by Ashesh Goplani
docs(10): create phase plan for learnings promotion
docs(phase-10): add research and validation strategy

- Added exit 137 section to conductor CLAUDE.md with 5 mitigations
- Added GSD-specific exit 137 guidance to gsd-conductor SKILL.md
- Updated STATE.md and ROADMAP.md for phase 9 completion

Committed by Ashesh Goplani

docs(09-01): complete exit 137 investigation plan

- SUMMARY: Root cause traced to Claude Code, not fixable in agent-deck
- STATE: Phase 9 in progress, 3 decisions recorded
- ROADMAP: Phase 9 at 1/2, phases 7-8 marked complete
- REQUIREMENTS: PROC-01 marked complete

docs(09-01): investigate exit 137 SIGKILL root cause

- Trace signal chain: agent-deck -> tmux send-keys -> PTY -> Claude Code -> SIGKILL
- Root cause: Claude Code kills Bash tool children when new input arrives at composer
- Not fixable in agent-deck (Claude Code design choice)
- Document 6 mitigation strategies including --wait flag and status gating
docs(09): create phase plan for process stability investigation
docs(phase-08): complete phase execution

docs(08-02): complete CLI fixes plan

Committed by Ashesh Goplani
docs(08-01): complete heartbeat fixes plan

feat(08-02): improve help text for --no-parent, -c, and set-parent

- --no-parent help now mentions 'session set-parent' as recovery mechanism
- Added -c shorthand example in add command usage
- set-parent help explains compatibility with --no-parent sessions

Committed by Ashesh Goplani

feat(08-01): fix heartbeat group scoping, interval=0 semantics, and setup guard

- Heartbeat script now references conductor's own group ({NAME}), not all profile sessions
- Added enabled-config guard in heartbeat script (queries conductor status before sending)
- GetHeartbeatInterval returns 0 when interval=0 (disabled), 15 for negative (default)
- Conductor setup skips heartbeat daemon when interval=0
- Updated TUI clear-on-compact heartbeat to use group-scoped message
- Updated existing TestGetHeartbeatInterval to match new interval=0 semantics

feat(08-02): add session death detection to waitForCompletion

- Track consecutiveErrors counter, threshold at 5 consecutive GetStatus failures
- Return ("error", nil) on session death instead of polling indefinitely
- Reset counter on any successful GetStatus call (transient errors recover)
- Prevents --wait from hanging when tmux session dies during processing
- Fix pre-existing unused variable in home.go (vet blocker)

Committed by Ashesh Goplani

test(08-02): add failing test for session death detection

- TestWaitForCompletion_SessionDeath: verifies 5+ consecutive errors return ("error", nil)
- TestWaitForCompletion_TransientRecovery: verifies recovery from fewer than 5 errors
- TestReorderArgsForFlagParsing_CmdAndGroup: verifies -c and -g flags parse correctly together

Committed by Ashesh Goplani

test(08-01): add failing tests for heartbeat group scoping and interval semantics

- TestConductorHeartbeatScript_GroupScoped: asserts group-scoped message and enabled guard
- TestGetHeartbeatInterval_ZeroMeansDisabled: table-driven test for 0/negative/positive intervals
docs(08-heartbeat-cli-fixes): create phase plan
docs(08): add research and validation strategy
docs(08): research phase domain

docs(07-02): complete send reliability integration tests plan

Committed by Ashesh Goplani

test(07-02): add send reliability integration tests for real tmux

- TestSend_EnterRetryOnRealTmux: baseline end-to-end send via cat session
- TestSend_RapidSuccessiveSends: two rapid sends both deliver without drops
- TestSend_CodexReadinessSimulation: PromptDetector false before codex> prompt, true after, send delivers

Committed by Ashesh Goplani

docs(07-01): complete send verification consolidation plan

- SUMMARY.md with task commits, deviations, and self-check
- STATE.md updated with position, decisions, session info
- ROADMAP.md updated with 07-01 completion
- REQUIREMENTS.md: SEND-01 and SEND-02 marked complete

Committed by Ashesh Goplani

fix(07-01): harden Enter retry loop and add Codex readiness detection

SEND-01: More aggressive Enter retry in early window
- sendWithRetryTarget nudges Enter every iteration for first 5 retries
  (was every 3rd iteration) to catch bracketed paste timing failures
- Ambiguous-state Enter budget increased from 2 to 4 for slower TUIs
- Identical changes applied to sendMessageWhenReady in instance.go

SEND-02: Gate Codex sends on prompt readiness
- waitForAgentReady now polls for codex> prompt before allowing sends
- sendMessageWhenReady mirrors the same Codex readiness check
- Uses existing PromptDetector("codex") for consistent detection

Tests updated to reflect new retry cadence + 2 new tests added

Committed by Ashesh Goplani

feat(07-01): consolidate send verification into internal/send package

- Create internal/send/send.go with 7 exported functions (HasUnsentPastedPrompt,
  HasUnsentComposerPrompt, CurrentComposerPrompt, HasCurrentComposerPrompt,
  NormalizePromptText, IsComposerDividerLine, ParsePromptFromComposerBlock)
- Create internal/send/send_test.go with comprehensive tests for all functions
- Remove duplicated function definitions from session_cmd.go and instance.go
- Update callers in both files to use send.* package references
- Remove migrated tests from session_send_test.go and instance_test.go

fix(update): handle nested binary paths in release tarballs

extractBinaryFromTarGz checked header.Name == "agent-deck" which fails
when GoReleaser wraps files in a directory (e.g. "agent-deck_0.22.0_darwin_arm64/agent-deck").
Use filepath.Base() to match regardless of directory nesting.

releases.name: v0.25.1
releases.body:
## Agent Deck v0.25.1

Terminal session manager for AI coding agents.

### Installation

**Homebrew (recommended):**
```bash
brew install asheshgoplani/tap/agent-deck
```

**Quick Install:**
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck/main/install.sh | bash
```

**Go Install:**
```bash
go install github.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck/cmd/agent-deck@v0.25.1
```

## Changelog
* 8e0dc792189677f64dc70cfe11b9ef51815cc8a3 feat(ui): expose custom tools in settings panel

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck/compare/v0.25.0...v0.25.1

releases.name: v0.25.0
releases.body:
## Agent Deck v0.25.0

Terminal session manager for AI coding agents.

### Installation

**Homebrew (recommended):**
```bash
brew install asheshgoplani/tap/agent-deck
```

**Quick Install:**
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck/main/install.sh | bash
```

**Go Install:**
```bash
go install github.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck/cmd/agent-deck@v0.25.0
```

## Changelog
* 3f509fc32bf5f9d6c17b1107c9d39db1ae6460c7 feat(preview): add show_notes toggle to hide notes section
* b0cbb86e158b2ed329a55df26c23f8d1bc4f0ce2 feat(telegram-bot): enhance bot message handling with username management and mention stripping
* f95b4977232a569f4b0c6383c6d47206f4503d35 feat: enrich Slack bridge messages with sender and channel context
* 3864c78ec0df79513f1ab3c78e2a0e43f3276c97 feat: extend yolo mode toggle to Codex sessions (#315)
* 1e711c40bd621238bfee4e17b7be3ab0615b7d2e fix(conductor): scope bridge heartbeats by group
* 22af553143dfac544e4e132d6da0c15c252f6951 fix(preview): hide notes key hint and disable editor when show_notes is false
* 48c74b038c571a7c6ce3f9b02369e7d8e4ffd918 fix(preview): preserve show_notes behavior across help and settings
* 8022718e8fe8f1d4f62c9c8ed025c3624aa99feb fix(remote): add restart, close, and delete actions for remote sessions
* f9e0a95ac924d045d5035342d1d6b5351fcc16d9 fix: add StatusStopped to prevent conductor from restarting intentionally closed sessions
* 660aed43ba80f21f4b5cd951ec280352e2dfb3e2 fix: add negative cache TTL, stable IDs, and concurrent resolution
* 178fa53b21e51d02192489a72e83c7808a1ee21f fix: make yolo hotkey migration deterministic
* d790d8cedb5930c0b14ff3a95e27841e5d19d5bc gemini-hooks: add install flow and hook-based session/status sync
* 0711e6e9cd9eb69480f9b35a85f31240a83cf525 hook: persist session-id anchor as read-time fallback
* a4693fde8854bfa885ff50e5bc786827e51e3b75 perf: hoist regexp.MustCompile calls to package-level vars

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck/compare/v0.24.1...v0.25.0

releases.name: v0.23.0
releases.body:
## Agent Deck v0.23.0

Terminal session manager for AI coding agents.

### Installation

**Homebrew (recommended):**
```bash
brew install asheshgoplani/tap/agent-deck
```

**Quick Install:**
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck/main/install.sh | bash
```

**Go Install:**
```bash
go install github.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck/cmd/agent-deck@v0.23.0
```

### What's New

**Milestone v1.1 Integration Testing complete** (38 integration tests across 6 phases):

#### Added
- Status detection integration tests: real tmux status transition cycles, pattern detection, and tool config verification.
- Conductor pipeline integration tests: send-to-child delivery, cross-session event write-watch, heartbeat round-trips, and chunked send delivery.
- Edge case integration tests: skills discover-attach verification.

#### Fixed
- Handle nested binary paths in release tarballs so self-update works with both flat and directory-wrapped archives.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck/compare/v0.22.0...v0.23.0

readme_html:
Agent Deck

Your AI agent command center

Features . Conductor . Install . Quick Start . Docs . Discord . FAQ

Option 1: Claude Code Skill (recommended for Claude Code users)

/plugin marketplace add asheshgoplani/agent-deck
/plugin install agent-deck@agent-deck-help

Then ask: "How do I set up MCP pooling?"

Option 2: OpenCode (has built-in Claude skill compatibility)

# Create skill directory
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/agent-deck/references

# Download skill and references
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck/main/skills/agent-deck/SKILL.md \
  > ~/.claude/skills/agent-deck/SKILL.md
for f in cli-reference config-reference tui-reference troubleshooting; do
  curl -sL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck/main/skills/agent-deck/references/${f}.md" \
    > ~/.claude/skills/agent-deck/references/${f}.md
done

OpenCode will auto-discover the skill from ~/.claude/skills/.

Option 3: Any LLM (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, etc.)

Read https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck/main/llms-full.txt
and answer: How do I fork a session?

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e4f55917-435c-45ba-92cc-89737d0d1401

The Problem

Running Claude Code on 10 projects? OpenCode on 5 more? Another agent somewhere in the background?

Managing multiple AI sessions gets messy fast. Too many terminal tabs. Hard to track what's running, what's waiting, what's done. Switching between projects means hunting through windows.

The Solution

Agent Deck is mission control for your AI coding agents.

One terminal. All your agents. Complete visibility.

  • See everything at a glance — running, waiting, or idle status for every agent instantly
  • Switch in milliseconds — jump between any session with a single keystroke
  • Stay organized — groups, search, notifications, and git worktrees keep everything manageable

Features

Fork Sessions

Try different approaches without losing context. Fork any Claude conversation instantly. Each fork inherits the full conversation history.

  • Press f for quick fork, F to customize name/group
  • Fork your forks to explore as many branches as you need

MCP Manager

Attach MCP servers without touching config files. Need web search? Browser automation? Toggle them on per project or globally. Agent Deck handles the restart automatically.

  • Press m to open, Space to toggle, Tab to cycle scope (LOCAL/GLOBAL), type to jump
  • Define your MCPs once in ~/.agent-deck/config.toml, then toggle per session — see Configuration Reference

Skills Manager

Attach/detach Claude skills per project with a managed pool workflow.

  • Press s to open Skills Manager for a Claude session
  • Available list is pool-only (~/.agent-deck/skills/pool) to keep attach/detach deterministic
  • Apply writes project state to .agent-deck/skills.toml and materializes into .claude/skills
  • Type-to-jump is supported in the dialog (same pattern as MCP Manager)

MCP Socket Pool

Running many sessions? Socket pooling shares MCP processes across all sessions via Unix sockets, reducing MCP memory usage by 85-90%. Connections auto-recover from MCP crashes in ~3 seconds via a reconnecting proxy. Enable with pool_all = true in config.toml.

Search

Press / to fuzzy-search across all sessions. Filter by status with ! (running), @ (waiting), # (idle), $ (error). Press G for global search across all Claude conversations.

Status Detection

Smart polling detects what every agent is doing right now:

Status Symbol What It Means
Running ● green Agent is actively working
Waiting ◐ yellow Needs your input
Idle ○ gray Ready for commands
Error ✕ red Something went wrong

Notification Bar

Waiting sessions appear right in your tmux status bar. Press Ctrl+b, release, then press 1–6 to jump directly to them.

⚡ [1] frontend [2] api [3] backend

Git Worktrees

Multiple agents can work on the same repo without conflicts. Each worktree is an isolated working directory with its own branch.

  • agent-deck add . -c claude --worktree feature/a --new-branch creates a session in a new worktree
  • agent-deck add . --worktree feature/b -b --location subdirectory places the worktree under .worktrees/ inside the repo
  • agent-deck worktree finish "My Session" merges the branch, removes the worktree, and deletes the session
  • agent-deck worktree cleanup finds and removes orphaned worktrees

Configure the default worktree location in ~/.agent-deck/config.toml:

[worktree]
default_location = "subdirectory"  # "sibling" (default), "subdirectory", or a custom path

sibling creates worktrees next to the repo (repo-branch). subdirectory creates them inside it (repo/.worktrees/branch). A custom path like ~/worktrees or /tmp/worktrees creates repo-namespaced worktrees at <path>/<repo_name>/<branch>. The --location flag overrides the config per session.

Docker Sandbox

Run sessions inside isolated Docker containers. The project directory is bind-mounted read-write, so agents work on your code while the rest of the system stays protected.

  • Check "Run in Docker sandbox" when creating a session, or set default_enabled = true in config
  • Press T on a sandboxed session to open a container shell
  • agent-deck try "task description" runs a one-shot sandboxed session

Host tool auth (Claude, Gemini, Codex, etc.) is automatically shared into containers via shared sandbox directories — no re-authentication needed. On macOS, Keychain credentials are extracted too.

[docker]
default_enabled = true
mount_ssh = true

See the Docker Sandbox Guide for the full reference including overlay details, custom images, and troubleshooting.

Conductor

Conductors are persistent Claude Code sessions that monitor and orchestrate all your other sessions. They watch for sessions that need help, auto-respond when confident, and escalate to you when they can't. Optionally connect Telegram and/or Slack for remote control.

Create as many conductors as you need per profile:

# First-time setup (asks about Telegram/Slack, then creates the conductor)
agent-deck -p work conductor setup ops --description "Ops monitor"

# Add more conductors to the same profile (no prompts)
agent-deck -p work conductor setup infra --description "Infra watcher"
agent-deck conductor setup personal --description "Personal project monitor"

Each conductor gets its own directory, identity, and settings:

~/.agent-deck/conductor/
├── CLAUDE.md           # Shared knowledge (CLI ref, protocols, rules)
├── bridge.py           # Bridge daemon (Telegram/Slack, if configured)
├── ops/
│   ├── CLAUDE.md       # Identity: "You are ops, a conductor for the work profile"
│   ├── meta.json       # Config: name, profile, description
│   ├── state.json      # Runtime state
│   └── task-log.md     # Action log
└── infra/
    ├── CLAUDE.md
    └── meta.json

CLI commands:

agent-deck conductor list                    # List all conductors
agent-deck conductor list --profile work     # Filter by profile
agent-deck conductor status                  # Health check (all)
agent-deck conductor status ops              # Health check (specific)
agent-deck conductor teardown ops            # Stop a conductor
agent-deck conductor teardown --all --remove # Remove everything

Telegram bridge (optional): Connect a Telegram bot for mobile monitoring. The bridge routes messages to specific conductors using a name: message prefix:

ops: check the frontend session      → routes to conductor-ops
infra: restart all error sessions    → routes to conductor-infra
/status                              → aggregated status across all profiles

Slack bridge (optional): Connect a Slack bot for channel-based monitoring via Socket Mode. The bot listens in a dedicated channel and replies in threads to keep the channel clean. Uses the same name: message routing, plus slash commands:

ops: check the frontend session      → routes to conductor-ops (reply in thread)
/ad-status                           → aggregated status across all profiles
/ad-sessions                         → list all sessions
/ad-restart [name]                   → restart a conductor
/ad-help                             → list available commands
  1. Create a Slack app at api.slack.com/apps
  2. Enable Socket Mode → generate an app-level token (xapp-...)
  3. Under OAuth & Permissions, add bot scopes: chat:write, channels:history, channels:read, app_mentions:read
  4. Under Event Subscriptions, subscribe to bot events: message.channels, app_mention
  5. If using slash commands, create: /ad-status, /ad-sessions, /ad-restart, /ad-help
  6. Install the app to your workspace
  7. Invite the bot to your channel (/invite @botname)
  8. Run agent-deck conductor setup <name> and enter your bot token (xoxb-...), app token (xapp-...), and channel ID (C01234...)

Both Telegram and Slack can run simultaneously — the bridge daemon handles both concurrently and relays responses on-demand, plus periodic heartbeat alerts to configured platforms.

Built-in status-driven notifications: conductor setup also installs a transition notifier daemon (agent-deck notify-daemon) that watches status transitions and sends parent nudges when child sessions move running -> waiting|error|idle.

Heartbeat-driven monitoring: heartbeats still run on the configured interval (default 15 minutes) as a secondary safety net. If a conductor response includes NEED:, the bridge forwards that alert to Telegram and/or Slack.

Permission prompts during automation: if a conductor keeps pausing on permission requests, set [claude].allow_dangerous_mode = true (or dangerous_mode = true) in ~/.agent-deck/config.toml, then run agent-deck session restart conductor-<name>. See Troubleshooting.

Legacy external watcher scripts: optional only. ~/.agent-deck/events/ is not required for notification routing.

Launching sessions from inside a conductor:

# Inherit current conductor as parent (default when AGENT_DECK_SESSION_ID is set)
agent-deck -p work launch . -t "child-task" -c claude -m "Do task"

# Keep parent notifications and still force a custom group
agent-deck -p work launch . -t "review-phantom" -g ard -c claude -m "Review dataset"

# Tool command with extra args is supported directly
agent-deck -p work launch . -c "codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox"

When --cmd includes extra args, agent-deck auto-wraps the tool command so args are preserved reliably. Use --no-parent only when you explicitly want to disable parent routing/notifications.

Multi-Tool Support

Agent Deck works with any terminal-based AI tool:

Tool Integration Level
Claude Code Full (status, MCP, fork, resume)
Gemini CLI Full (status, MCP, resume)
OpenCode Status detection, organization
Codex Status detection, organization
Cursor (terminal) Status detection, organization
Custom tools Configurable via [tools.*] in config.toml

Installation

Works on: macOS, Linux, Windows (WSL)

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck/main/install.sh | bash

Then run: agent-deck

Homebrew

brew install asheshgoplani/tap/agent-deck

Go

go install github.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck/cmd/agent-deck@latest

From Source

git clone https://github.com/asheshgoplani/agent-deck.git && cd agent-deck && make install

Claude Code Skill

Install the agent-deck skill for AI-assisted session management:

/plugin marketplace add asheshgoplani/agent-deck
/plugin install agent-deck@agent-deck

agent-deck uninstall              # Interactive uninstall
agent-deck uninstall --keep-data  # Remove binary only, keep sessions

See Troubleshooting for full details.

Quick Start

agent-deck                        # Launch TUI
agent-deck add . -c claude        # Add current dir with Claude
agent-deck session fork my-proj   # Fork a Claude session
agent-deck mcp attach my-proj exa # Attach MCP to session
agent-deck skill attach my-proj docs --source pool --restart # Attach skill + restart
agent-deck web                    # Start web UI on http://127.0.0.1:8420

Web Mode

Open the left menu + browser terminal UI:

agent-deck web

Read-only browser mode (output only):

agent-deck web --read-only

Change the listen address (default: 127.0.0.1:8420):

agent-deck web --listen 127.0.0.1:9000

Protect API + WebSocket access with a bearer token:

agent-deck web --token my-secret
# then open: http://127.0.0.1:8420/?token=my-secret

Key Shortcuts

Key Action
Enter Attach to session
n New session
f / F Fork (quick / dialog)
m MCP Manager
s Skills Manager (Claude)
M Move session to group
S Settings
/ / G Search / Global search
r Restart session
d Delete
S Settings
T Container shell (sandboxed sessions)
? Full help

See TUI Reference for all shortcuts and CLI Reference for all commands.

Documentation

Guide What's Inside
CLI Reference Commands, flags, scripting examples
Configuration config.toml, MCP setup, custom tools, socket pool, skills registry paths, docker
Docker Sandbox Containers, overlays, custom images, troubleshooting
TUI Reference Keyboard shortcuts, status indicators, navigation
Troubleshooting Common issues, debugging, recovery, uninstalling

Additional resources:

  • CONTRIBUTING.md — how to contribute
  • CHANGELOG.md — release history
  • llms-full.txt — full context for LLMs

Updates

Agent Deck checks for updates automatically.

  • Standalone/manual install: run agent-deck update to install.
  • Homebrew install: run brew upgrade asheshgoplani/tap/agent-deck.
  • Optional: set auto_update = true in config.toml for automatic update prompts.

FAQ

Agent Deck adds AI-specific intelligence on top of tmux: smart status detection (knows when Claude is thinking vs. waiting), session forking with context inheritance, MCP management, global search across conversations, and organized groups. Think of it as tmux plus AI awareness.

Yes, via WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux). Install WSL, then run the installer inside WSL. WSL2 is recommended for full feature support including MCP socket pooling.

Yes. Set a global Claude config dir, then add optional per-profile overrides in ~/.agent-deck/config.toml:

[claude]
config_dir = "~/.claude"             # Global default

[profiles.work.claude]
config_dir = "~/.claude-work"        # Work account

Run with the target profile:

agent-deck -p work

You can verify which Claude config path is active with:

agent-deck hooks status
agent-deck hooks status -p work

See Configuration Reference for full details.

No. Agent Deck creates its own tmux sessions with the prefix agentdeck_*. Your existing sessions are untouched. The installer backs up your ~/.tmux.conf before adding optional config, and you can skip it with --skip-tmux-config.

Development

make build    # Build
make test     # Test
make lint     # Lint

See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.

Star History

If Agent Deck saves you time, give us a star! It helps others discover the project.
